_comment: All rates are evidence-based guidelines (ISSN / Burke et al.). Adjust as needed.

carb_rates_g_per_hour._comment: Base carb intake targets by race type, indexed by effort tier

_comment: Secondary multiplier applied on top of the effort-indexed base carb rate.
easy      0.85 — at sub-threshold efforts fat oxidation contributes more
(~50-60% VO2max); carbohydrate demand is reduced, so the base
rate is scaled down to avoid over-fuelling and GI distress.
race_pace 1.00 — the base rates in carb_rates_g_per_hour are calibrated
for race-pace effort; no additional adjustment needed.
hard      1.15 — above threshold (~85%+ VO2max) muscles rely almost
exclusively on glycogen. A 15% uplift targets higher turnover
and supports delayed fatigue in shorter, intense efforts.

condition_modifiers._comment: Sodium and fluid uplifts for environmental conditions

sodium_targets_mg_per_hour._comment: Base sodium target before condition modifier. Scales with body weight.

fluid_targets_ml_per_hour._comment: Base fluid target before condition modifier. Scales with body weight.

gender_modifiers._comment: Small downward adjustment for female athletes (lower avg sweat rate)

timing_rules._comment: When to consume each product type relative to race start (minutes)

during.note: First intake at ~20 min, then every 30 min

caffeine_rules.note: Caffeine gels replace one plain gel slot when caffeine_ok = true

training_mode._comment: In training mode reduce carb targets to train the gut at lower doses
